result.meeting_context: The Mountain View Human Relations Commission held a virtual meeting expected to be its last remote session, welcoming three new commissioners and reviewing the FY2022–23 work plan. Commissioners received subcommittee updates, assigned new members to committees, scheduled the housing storytelling event, and elected the 2023 Chair and Vice Chair. Staff also outlined upcoming compliance requirements and the return to in‑person meetings in February.

points.speaker: Ida Rose Sylvester
points.text: The Commission conducted this meeting virtually and anticipates it will be the last remote session after three years of online meetings, with all commissioners participating by videoconference and no physical meeting location.
points.source: Ida Rose Sylvester : All members of the Human Relations Commission will participate in the meeting by video conference with no physical meeting location.

points.speaker: Ida Rose Sylvester
points.text: No public comments were received for this meeting; staff reported no emails by the 5:00 PM deadline, and there were no members of the public present on the webinar during the oral communications period.
points.source: Ida Rose Sylvester : I see no members of the public on the line, so we will not have to, you cannot take feedback.

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Laurel James informed the Commission that Commissioner Raigstraw resigned from the Human Relations Commission effective immediately and would not be joining the meeting.
points.source: Laurel James : I received a notice from Commissioner Raigstraw this evening that she'll be resigning from the HRC effective immediately.

points.speaker: Ida Rose Sylvester
points.text: Ida Rose Sylvester welcomed new commissioners Jocelyn Baird, Molly Smith, and Eric Poicon, noting it may be the greatest number of new HRC members seated at a single meeting and emphasizing the opportunity to evolve and grow the Commission’s work.
points.source: Ida Rose Sylvester : It's also extremely exciting to welcome our new members Jocelyn, Molly and Eric Poicon.

points.speaker: Ida Rose Sylvester
points.text: The Commission approved the December 1, 2022 regular meeting minutes following a motion and roll call vote in which all present commissioners voted yes.

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Laurel James reviewed the FY2022–23 work plan (July 1, 2022 to June 30, 2023). Ongoing items include HRC participation in the annual Spring Family Parade (expected April; date forthcoming) and attendance at Council Neighborhoods Committee meetings, with spring dates to be shared when available.
points.source: Laurel James : That event will be upcoming in April. Once there's a date available, I will let you know when to expect to attend that event.

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: She noted the Commission received the Multicultural Engagement Program update in the fall and an update on the CDBG and HOME funding request process, which will begin at the next HRC meeting.
points.source: Laurel James : We also received and update on the CDBG and home funding request process for this year which will begin at the next HRC meeting.

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: The housing insecurity storytelling event is scheduled for May 25, 2023 at the Mountain View Community Center (Redwood Room). The subcommittee currently includes Ida Rose Sylvester and Xavier Webb, with a vacancy after a former member’s departure.
points.source: Laurel James : The event related to this Housing and Security Project Storytelling project is currently scheduled for May 25th, 2023 and it will be held at the Mountain View Community Center in the Redwood room.

points.speaker: Ida Rose Sylvester
points.text: Ida Rose Sylvester outlined a two-part format: mixed roundtable discussions including unhoused and housed participants without identifying housing status, followed by testimonials and Q&A from unstably housed speakers. The subcommittee has worked with Reach Potential Movement and plans additional partner outreach.
points.source: Ida Rose Sylvester : We will potentially mix our unhoused people... in tables like round tables... And then the second part of the event would be a chance for people for the unstably house to get up and talk about their experiences.

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: The mental health community dialogue is in progress, led by Nirvana Nwokido and Ida Rose Sylvester, and tentatively targeted for late June 2023 (date TBD). Planning is focused on format, venue, and participation.
points.source: Laurel James : The event is tentatively scheduled for late June 2023, although no date has been selected yet.

points.speaker: Nirvana Nwokido
points.text: Nirvana Nwokido reported two subcommittee meetings have explored panels, presenters, and personal testimonies, plus compiling local resources and partnering with organizations. She emphasized destigmatizing mental health and noted there is no city funding for the effort.
points.source: Nirvana Nwokido : So the city does not have any kind of funding for for this. But it's something that we feel is very critical... we want to bring this to our community and come up with ways to provide some kind of safe place.

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: The bystander intervention training project, requested by the Council’s Race, Equity and Inclusion Subcommittee and carried over from FY2021–22, is in progress. Following turnover, Commissioner Xavier Webb is the current subcommittee member, and additional seats were open prior to new assignments.
points.source: Laurel James : Currently, I believe the only member of that subcommittee is Commissioner Webb, as the other two members... are no longer in the HRC, and this is a item that was carried forward from the Fiscal Year 21 to 22 plan.

points.speaker: Xavier Webb
points.text: Xavier Webb said the subcommittee will leverage established external curricula and resources rather than creating training from scratch, using notes from prior commissioners and developing outreach to adapt offerings for the community.
points.source: Xavier Webb : We're definitely not recreating the wheel. What we were discussing before was other organizations that have already done this and we were looking into what they were doing or the cost of those.

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Subcommittee seats were finalized: Housing & Security—Ida Rose Sylvester, Xavier Webb, Eric Poicon; Mental Health—Ida Rose Sylvester, Nirvana Nwokido, Molly Smith; Bystander Intervention—Xavier Webb, Jocelyn Baird, Eric Poicon. Assignments run through June or project completion as part of the current work plan.
points.source: Laurel James : Bystander... Commissioners Webb, Baird and Poicon; ... Mental Health... Vice Chair Sylvester, Commissioner Wilkidu, and Commissioner Smith; ... Housing and Security... Vice Chair Sylvester, Commissioner Webb and Commissioner Poicon.

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Staff reminded commissioners to attend the mid‑January Brown Act refresher training and to complete Form 700 filings via the City’s Netfile system (emails from Netfile are legitimate). Staff offered to verify any messages if needed.
points.source: Laurel James : This is a reminder that the City Clerk's Office has sent you invitation for Brown Act Refresher training for mid January... you will receive an e-mail from a platform called Netfile... That is not spam.

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: The next HRC meeting will be held in person at City Hall on Thursday, February 2, 2023 at 6:30 PM in the Plaza Conference Room (2nd floor).
points.source: Laurel James : The next HRC meeting will not be virtual. It will be in person at City Hall on, I believe it's Thursday, February the 2nd at 6:30 PM.

points.speaker: Ida Rose Sylvester
points.text: Ida Rose Sylvester thanked participants and welcomed the new commissioners; commissioners, including Xavier Webb and Nirvana Nwokido, expressed enthusiasm about working together. The meeting adjourned to February 2, 2023 at 6:30 PM at Mountain View City Hall.
points.source: Ida Rose Sylvester : This meeting is adjourned to our next regular meeting which is scheduled for February 2nd, 2023 at 6:30 PM... at Mountain View City Hall.

decisions.text: Hold the housing insecurity storytelling event on May 25, 2023 at the Mountain View Community Center (Redwood Room).
decisions.source: Laurel James : The event related to this Housing and Security Project Storytelling project is currently scheduled for May 25th, 2023 and it will be held at the Mountain View Community Center in the Redwood room.

decisions.assignee: Housing and Security Subcommittee (Sylvester, Webb, Poicon)
decisions.text: Membership set as Ida Rose Sylvester, Xavier Webb, and Eric Poicon through June 2023 or project completion.
decisions.source: Laurel James : Housing and Security would be comprised of you, Vice Chair Sylvester, Commissioner Webb and Commissioner Poicon.

decisions.text: Membership set as Ida Rose Sylvester, Nirvana Nwokido, and Molly Smith through June 2023 or project completion.
decisions.source: Laurel James : The Mental Health Subcommittee would include yourself, Vice Chair Sylvester, Commissioner Wilkidu, and Commissioner Smith.

decisions.text: Membership set as Xavier Webb, Jocelyn Baird, and Eric Poicon through June 2023 or project completion.
decisions.source: Laurel James : Bystander Intervention Subcommittee would be comprised of Commissioners Webb, Baird and Poicon.

decisions.text: Elected HRC Chair for 2023, responsible for meeting agendas and facilitation.
decisions.source: Laurel James : We’ll be voting on whether or not to elect her as chair of the HRC.

decisions.text: Elected HRC Vice Chair for 2023, to support the Chair and serve in their absence.
decisions.source: Laurel James : For the election of the Vice Chair, we will be voting on the nomination of Commissioner Volkito.

decisions.text: Provide commissioners the Spring Family Parade date once finalized (event anticipated in April).
decisions.source: Laurel James : That event will be upcoming in April. Once there's a date available, I will let you know when to expect to attend that event.

decisions.text: Share the spring Council Neighborhoods Committee meeting schedule with commissioners when available.
decisions.source: Laurel James : Once I have the Spring Meeting scheduled... I will provide it to you so that you can make plans accordingly.
